title: Medical Care Expense Assistance * Breast Cancer - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Medical Care Expense Assistance * Breast Cancer 9 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Breast Cancer Cancer Cervical Cancer Stillbirth Women Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. Applicant 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Vary by site Email (800) 699-9075 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH ASHLAND HEALTH CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. The program does not look at gender identity or expression.
